snapshot.scope: Manual review binds public and documentation surfaces, control manifests, verifier code, and shared executable schemas. Focused automated gates execute current implementation and test evidence on every run.

reviewers.independence: Fresh read-only reviewer on the exact 101-file B5.12/LLM Wiki candidate; independently checked the TECH reference row, upstream tag/license/security surface, Owner/trace status changes, navigation and lifecycle controls, evidence selectors, budgets, and active-development compatibility without editing files.

Fresh read-only PRD/trace/evidence reviewer on the exact 101-file candidate; independently traced B5.12 Owner wording, v3 fixtures, negative controls, executable selectors, status/open-gap boundaries, stable IDs, PRD scope, and the 1,050-claim semantic lock without editing files.

Fresh read-only active-development and governance reviewer on the exact 101-file candidate; independently checked snapshot coverage, phase and stable-ID continuity, exact status-promotion scope, Owner separation, reference-only adoption cost, residual P5 work, and capacity without editing files.

dimensions.summary: All 57 Markdown and two distribution files remain uniquely governed; 114 local links and 45 anchors resolve; LLM Wiki has one canonical TECH registry row and no dependency or trace projection, while task packs and lifecycle reclamation remain explicit.

Quality owns the executable B5.12 evaluation contract, the Playbook owns P5 phase state, acceptance owns Requirement/Exit status and evidence, and TECH owns the LLM Wiki reference row; CON-001 through CON-004 pass with no duplicated normative owner or incompatible projection.

B5.12's seven deterministic multilingual cases and four negative controls precisely support PIGE-EVAL-001 and E5.04 as verified; PIGE-PROMPT-001 and E5.03 correctly remain partial pending full Pi tool/Permission Broker runtime proof. All 151 P0 items, 98 Requirements, 140 Builds, 96 Exits, stable IDs, and 1,050 semantic claims remain controlled.

The two exact status promotions close only B5.12/E5.04 and do not reorder P5, unlock later phases, change dependencies, or narrow remaining P5 work; the LLM Wiki row is static research with no manifest, package, runtime, fixture, or acceptance role.

LEN-001 through LEN-005 pass with 4,190 entry words, 57 Markdown files, 22,810 lines, 162,804 words, 1,208,114 bytes, 5,479 trace lines, zero duplicate groups, and 14 mutation cases; the single compact LLM Wiki row adds no machine projection, though capacity remains tight.

verification.command: npm run verify:docs && npm run verify:decisions && npm run verify:ui-visual && npm run verify:contracts && npm run verify:dependencies
verification.evidence: Document inventory, 114 local links, 45 anchors, whitespace, parser manifests, 76 decisions, 12 UI tokens, 8 structural rules, 448 visual combinations, CON-001 through CON-004, Owner-negative cases, and 45 dependency records with zero waivers passed.

verification.command: npm run verify:prd-contract && npm run verify:traceability
verification.evidence: The 151/9/10 scope baseline, 98 Requirements, 140 Builds, 96 Exits, 32 Deferred records, 1,050 semantic claims, 13 PRD negative cases, TRC-001 through TRC-005, and 22 traceability false-positive mutations passed; the exact B5.12 contract supports verified PIGE-EVAL-001 and E5.04 while adjacent prompt controls remain partial.
All 52 test files and 303 tests passed; package and desktop type checks passed on the reviewed state.
The focused two-file, seven-test B5.12 and untrusted-source evaluation passed on the exact reviewed candidate.

All five leanness checks passed: 4,190 entry words, zero unapproved short-line/list/table/multiline/fenced/declaration/URL/near-prose groups, 14 mutation cases, 57 Markdown files, 5,479 trace lines, and 9.04% line reduction.

coordination.compatibility: P0-P9 phases, M0-M7 milestones, and all stable Requirement/Build/Exit IDs remain unchanged. PIGE-EVAL-001 and E5.04 moved from partial to verified only under the exact B5.12 deterministic contract; PIGE-PROMPT-001 and E5.03 remain partial, P5 remains in progress, and the LLM Wiki TECH row remains reference-only with no dependency, acceptance, scope, or implementation-order effect.
coordination.acknowledgement: The development task acknowledged a one-row TECH §16.8 lock while B5.12 code, fixtures, tests, Quality, Playbook, acceptance, and semantic-lock work proceeded in disjoint ranges. The recipe stayed frozen until both slices reached one stable candidate; three fresh read-only reviewers then checked the exact 101-file snapshot before this single combined binding.

resolvedBlockers: Removed the recurring quality-line slogan from repository guidance and kept completion criteria as internal verification rules rather than repeated response copy.
Recast the PRD as an AI Coding Agent product contract with explicit authority, optimization order, default user model, observable states, product scenarios, risks, and Owner references.
Added bidirectional PRD-impact classes and propagation controls, then preserved all 151 P0 items while removing implementation-detail leakage and duplicate future-scope prose.

Removed conflicting parser/runtime types, repeated technical limits, directory trees, Settings/SQLite/prompt blocks, copied policy/test lists, duplicate references, and repeated short normative sentences in favor of one Owner plus narrow projections.

Replaced the false-pass duplicate check with strict schema-v2 LEN-002 coverage for short lines, lists, tables, mixed blocks, fenced content, declarations, external URLs, and ordered near prose; zero unapproved groups and 14 mutation cases now pass.

Registered Paperling, Crawl4AI, Microsoft MarkItDown, OfficeCLI, and LLM Wiki once each as pinned static research; none enters dependency or acceptance manifests, and each retains explicit no-reuse/no-runtime boundaries.

Preserved and independently reviewed the Clean-Room Visual System, then made its machine manifest both an explicit snapshot root and a verifier-required root so the manual review cannot silently omit it; structural checks still do not claim packaged screenshot acceptance.

Aligned Source Storage, Parser, Job, Prompt, Playbook, Quality, and acceptance evidence with the implemented third SourceRecord check, create-only note publication, conflict preservation, same-source recovery, and strict untrusted output while preserving partial status and every residual commit window.

Corrected the initial universal pre-Artifact-baseline wording for PDF OCR, accepted the reviewed semantic evolution from 1,040 through 1,046 to 1,050 claims, and restored dependency/UI checklist items to their proper Markdown section ownership before final review.

Completed the exact B5.12 deterministic contract with seven multilingual text/URL/PDF/PPTX/image-OCR cases and four negative controls, promoting only PIGE-EVAL-001 and E5.04 while preserving PIGE-PROMPT-001, E5.03, and P5 open scope.
nonBlockingRisks: P5 remains in progress: Source Page and Agent evidence checks still lack strict cross-process SourceRecord-to-note CAS across the check-to-link window, parent-swap-resistant destination binding, true cross-file transactions, and packaged-platform hard-link proof; Windows/Paddle, slide/media OCR, visible progress, and cooperative cancellation also remain open.

The entry budget has only 10 words of headroom, trace manifests use 5,479 of 5,500 lines, and governed documentation has only 696 words and 1,886 bytes of physical budget remaining; future additions must reclaim repetition or machine projection capacity in the same change.

TECH_ARCHITECTURE, PRD, and Decision Log remain large Owner documents; future Agents must continue section-targeted reads, while similarity checks remain complemented by Owner and focused contract gates.

Paperling, Crawl4AI, MarkItDown, OfficeCLI, and LLM Wiki pins are review-time facts; any experiment must repeat license, security, supply-chain, isolation, and platform checks. LLM Wiki remains GPL-3.0 static research only, and its code, assets, fixtures, runtime, API/MCP/Skill/shell, cloud-parser, secret-storage, and deletion patterns are not authorized for reuse.

B5.12 is a synthetic deterministic evaluation over static model outputs; it verifies the exact E5.04 contract but does not prove arbitrary provider-model quality, real parser/OCR fidelity, or packaged-platform behavior. Public URL/DOCX/PDF/PPTX/image-OCR adversarial cases do not replace full Pi tool/Permission Broker runtime proof, so PIGE-PROMPT-001 and E5.03 remain partial.

The Clean-Room label records a repository implementation constraint, not a legal conclusion; future UI work must not reread or copy external code, branded assets, fonts, exact compositions, or other protected expression and may still require specialist legal review.

The focused evidence does not yet include an exact positive selector for PDF OCR merging into the latest Source Record, nor dedicated assertions for the 16 KiB preview limit and temporary-file flags; current status correctly remains partial.

The UI visual verifier checks governed structural markers, not semantic Owner equivalence, accessibility, or packaged fidelity; real macOS/Windows captures and human review remain open, and baseline PNG/sidecar retention or pruning must be defined when the capture runner is implemented.
Any intentional PRD scope, Owner contract, evidence, status, or semantic-claim change still requires readable impact review and explicit lock regeneration rather than mechanical acceptance.
